title: Lithocarpus talangensis
intro: Lithocarpus talangensis, also known as Talang stone pine, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family (Fagaceae) native to Sumatra, Indonesia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus talangensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus talangensis is classified as a species of evergreen tree in the family Fagaceae. It belongs to the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of deciduous or evergreen trees found in tropical and subtropical regions. Lithocarpus talangensis is further classified as a member of the order Fagales, which comprises families of trees and shrubs that produce catkins and acorns. The species is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, specifically in Indonesia and Malaysia. Further classification of Lithocarpus talangensis includes its ranking as a member of the kingdom Plantae, and its scientific classification as a Fagaceae family member.
uses: Lithocarpus talangensis is a species of plant in the family Fagaceae.  Research on the plant indicates that its extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are believed to possess medicinal properties that can help reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The leaves are also used to treat fever and cough. However, more studies are needed to confirm the full range of its medicinal uses.

properties: Lithocarpus talangensis, a plant species native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant's leaves and bark are also thought to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against various pathogens. Additionally, Lithocarpus talangensis may have cardiovascular benefits, as some studies suggest it can help lower blood pressure and improve lipid profiles. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lithocarpus talangensis for medicinal purposes.
constituents: Lithocarpus talangensis contains several active constituents, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, which have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. These active compounds may contribute to the plant's traditional uses in folk medicine. Further research is needed to fully understand the specific mechanisms and effects of these constituents. The triterpenoids present in Lithocarpus talangensis may also exhibit cytotoxic and antiviral activities. Additional studies are required to elucidate the full range of active constituents and their potential applications.
